caption: The video appears to be a screen recording of an IRS webpage discussing Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) for IRAs and retirement plans. The page explains that RMDs cannot be rolled over to another IRA or retirement plan and provides a link to an RMD Comparison Chart. It highlights that Roth IRAs do not require distributions while the original owner is alive. The video then focuses on the section titled "RMD Calculations and 50% tax on missed distributions," which details the responsibilities of IRA trustees and plan administrators in reporting or calculating RMDs. It also mentions that IRA owners must calculate RMDs separately for each IRA they own, though they can withdraw the total amount from one or more IRAs. However, RMDs from workplace retirement plans must be taken separately from each plan. The video emphasizes the severe penalty of a 50% excise tax on any missed or insufficient distributions and notes that the IRS provides worksheets to help calculate RMDs. The cursor is visible, indicating user interaction with the webpage.
